{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Katayama shrine 01.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kameyama|WD=Q646858|1=Kameyama, Mie}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.89113888888889|N|136.33719444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} * * {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} (Also known as Suzuka Myojin or Suzuka Gongen) - Located in Kameyama City, Mie Prefecture. {{神社 |名称 = 片山神社 |image = [[File:Katayama shrine 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama City|1=Kameyama, Mie}}{{ill|Seki, Mie|en|Seki, Mie|WD=Q7447180|lt=Seki Town|1=Seki, Mie}}坂下636 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 53 | 緯度秒 = 28.1 | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 20 | 経度秒 = 13.9 |祭神 = {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.amigo.ne.jp/~chitonr2/jinjaichiran_kameyama.html|title=三重県の神社一覧 亀山市（20社）|publisher=三重県神社庁|accessdate=2018-2-9}}
{{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt_ja=瀬織津比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|lt_ja=氣吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86734190}} |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 鈴鹿大明神、鈴鹿明神、鈴鹿権現、鈴鹿神社、縣主の神社{{Refnest|group=注|『伊勢参宮名所図会』より}} |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Katayama shrine from Tōkaidō meisho zue.png|サムネイル|250px|right|東海道名所図会「鈴鹿社」]] '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} located at Sakasita 636, {{ill|Seki, Mie|en|Seki, Mie|WD=Q7447180|lt=Seki Town|1=Seki, Mie}}, {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama City|1=Kameyama, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. == Overview == It is situated on the {{ill|Suzuka Tunnel|ja|鈴鹿トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Tunnel|1=Suzuka tunnel (Shin-Meishin Expressway)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649475}} of {{ill|Japan National Route 1|en|Japan National Route 1|WD=Q1191191|lt=National Route 1|1=Japan National Route 1}}, which forms part of the border ({{ill|Prefectural Border|ja|県境|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Border|1=prefectural border|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582641}}) between {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, atop the {{ill|Suzuka Pass|ja|鈴鹿峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Pass|1=Suzuka Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649485}} from which one can overlook both the {{ill|Suzuka River|en|Suzuka River|WD=Q7651182|lt=Suzuka River|1=Suzuka River}} flowing toward {{ill|Ise Bay|en|Ise Bay|WD=Q1368308|lt=Ise Bay|1=Ise Bay}} and the {{ill|Tamura River|ja|田村川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura River|1=Tamura River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17172424}} flowing from {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}}{{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} The surrounding area is a sacred land blessed with abundant water. It is renowned as the site of the Suzuka Misogi, where the Saio ({{ill|Saiō|en|Saiō|WD=Q19841349|lt=Saio|1=Saiō}}) en route to {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} once stayed to perform misogi ({{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi|1=Misogi}}), and later became consecrated by shamanic practitioners ({{ill|Shamanic Practitioner|ja|巫覡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanic Practitioner|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu (shaman)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}}) performing purification rituals ({{ill|Purification Ritual|ja|祓え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祓|1=Harae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3127244}}). Within the precincts stand the monument commemorating the origin of Suzuka-ryu naginatajutsu – {{ill|Suzuka-ryu|ja|鈴鹿流|lt=Suzuka-ryu Naginatajutsu Origin|lt_ja=鈴鹿流薙刀術発生之地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka-ryu|1=Suzuka-ryū‎|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649496}} – and a monument honoring the filial piety of Bankichi. == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} ; Enshrined Deities * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt_ja=瀬織津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|lt_ja=氣吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86734190}} * {{ill|Hayasasurabime-no-kami|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurabime-no-kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Ooyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} == History == === Founding === The exact founding date is unknown. Originally, the three mountains of Mikoyama ({{ill|Suzuka-dake|ja|鈴鹿嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takenamada-dake|ja|武名嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenamada-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takahata-dake|ja|高幡嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) located on the eastern side of the Suzuka Pass were revered as a sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Sacred Mountain|1=Sacred mountains}}). On its central peak, {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} was enshrined, while on the left and right peaks {{ill|Ibuki-do-nushi|ja|伊吹戸主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibuki-do-nushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were respectively honored. However, a fire caused the transfer of the enshrinement to the old Suzuka Tondo Shrine. Subsequent repeated transfers due to frequent fires led to a unification of the shrine enshrining Yamato-hime – known as the Suzuka Shrine ({{ill|Suzuka Shrine|ja|鈴鹿社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – with the four deities, and later five additional deities including {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} were added. In the 2nd year of the Einin Era ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin Era|1=Einin}} 2, 1294), the shrine was relocated to the area of Tatsukami Saka and became the guardian shrine of Sakasita-juku ({{ill|Sakashita-juku|en|Sakashita-juku|WD=Q3033376|lt=Sakasita-juku|1=Sakashita-juku}}) enshrining Suzuka-hime. In the 40th year of the Meiji Era ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 40, 1907), the Tamura Shrine ({{ill|Tamura Shrine|ja|田村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine|qq=}}) near the Kagami Rock of Suzuka Pass was merged, forming the present structure. === General History === Due to its location on the border ({{ill|Provincial Border|ja|国境|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Border|1=State border|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12413618}}) between {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} and {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, {{ill|Suzuka Mountain|ja|鈴鹿山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Mountain|1=Q104539506|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539506}} was a key transportation hub since ancient times. Consequently, numerous legends concerning demons ({{ill|Oni|en|Oni|WD=Q730052|lt=Oni|1=Oni}}) and bandits ({{ill|Banditry|en|Banditry|WD=Q10968653|lt=Bandits|1=Banditry}}) have been handed down regarding the Suzuka Pass. Toward the end of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, the name “{{ill|Suzuka Gozen|en|Suzuka Gozen|WD=Q11649495|lt=Standing Eboshi|1=Suzuka Gozen}}” appears in the {{ill|Hobutsushu|en|Hobutsushu|WD=Q5875266|lt=Treasury Collection|1=Hobutsushu}}, and local legends relate that although {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} was ordered to subdue the Standing Eboshi, the two became husband and wife; after their deaths, the locals of the Suzuka Pass enshrined the Standing Eboshi as Suzuka Gomen and honored Tamuramaro in the Tamura Hall. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} In the {{ill|Nara Picture Book|ja|奈良絵本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Picture Book|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ‘Suzuka’, the Standing Eboshi of Suzuka is described as the Gongen of Suzuka, serving as the guardian deity ({{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}) of the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|WD=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (region)}}, protecting travelers in place of their own persons so that those journeying along the road would be spared misfortune. Furthermore, numerous small ritual vessels excavated in the vicinity of the Suzuka Pass suggest that travelers offered them in prayer for safe passage, indicating that the site functioned as a shrine festival remnant (and exhibiting characteristics of a Dosojin ({{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dosojin|1=Dōsojin}}) type deity). {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}} Much like the {{ill|Katsugun Jizō Bosatsu|ja|勝軍地蔵菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsugun Jizō Bosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}} enshrined on {{ill|Mount Atago|en|Mount Atago|WD=Q2975204|lt=Atago Mountain|1=Mount Atago}} in Kyoto, the Kagami Rock (also referred to as Kagamaseki) at the Suzuka Pass—regarded as an iwasaka ({{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwasaka|1=Iwakura rock}})—lies on the boundary between Kyoto ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|lt=Kyoto|lt_ja=京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}) and Tanba ({{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba|1=Tanba Province}}). There, by enshrining General Tamura within the rock, he was revered as a “general Jizō” in tandem with Suzuka Gongen, evidencing an ancient faith in Saino no Kami ({{ill|Saino no Kami|ja|塞の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saino no Kami|1=Q115978710|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q115978710}}). This belief later evolved into the legend of Sakanoue no Tamuramaro, as recounted in otogi-zōshi such as ‘Suzuka no Sōshi’ and ‘Standing Eboshi’, as well as in the {{ill|Okujōruri|ja|奥浄瑠璃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okujōruri|1=Okujoruri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361758}} text ‘Tamura Sandai-ki’, which narrates the tale of General Tamura and Suzuka Gomen as a divine couple battling the demon god ({{ill|Ootakemaru|en|Ootakemaru|WD=Q30926182|lt=Demon God|1=Ootakemaru}}). {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}} In the Shintō collection (‘{{ill|Shintōshū|en|Shintōshū|WD=Q2616931|lt=Shintō Shū|1=Shintōshū}}’), it is recorded that “the parents of Kakuwa-dono were also taught the principles of Shintō, and became the Suzuka Daimyōjin of the Iga Province Sannomiya”; however, since the Iga Sannomiya is identified with {{ill|Hataki Shrine|ja|波多岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this is suspected to be a misprint for the Ise Province Sannomiya. {{Refnest|group=注|『神道集』「児持山大明神の事」}} During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, the published work ‘{{ill|Ise Grand Shrine Famous Places Illustrated|ja|伊勢参宮名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine Famous Places Illustrated|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ in its “Suzuka Mountain” section depicts, separated by the Kagami Rock of the Suzuka Pass, a bolded “Suzuka Shrine” on the Ise side and, on the Ōmi side, a Tamura Myōjin (a shrine distinct from {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|ja|田村神社 (甲賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|1=Tamura Shrine (Koka City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576975}}), with an accompanying explanation stating that Suzuka Shrine was also known by alternate names such as Katayama Shrine and the bolded “Prefectural Shrine”. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} An inscribed stone pillar at the ruins of Tamura Shrine records that in the 40th year of the Meiji Era (1907) it was merged into Katayama Shrine; thus, the Tamura Shrine known as Tamura Myōjin during the Edo Period was incorporated into Katayama Shrine. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} In the 11th year of the Heisei Era (1999), the main hall was burned down due to arson. == Landmarks and Historic Sites == * Kagami Rock of Suzuka Mountain (Kagamaseki, Kagamihada) * The ruins of Tamura Shrine (also known as Tamura Hory or Tamura Myōjin) *: It is said that when Sakanoue no Tamuramaro deposited his bows and arrows ({{ill|Bows and Arrows|ja|弓箭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bows and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弓矢|1=Bow and arrow|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19827042}}), {{ill|Fumimuro Watamaro|ja|文室綿麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumimuro Watamaro|1=Fun'ya no Watamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2713178}} built a hall at that spot. {{Refnest|group=注|『三國地誌』巻之二十三 伊勢國 鈴鹿郡 神祠}} * The remains of the shrine festival site at Suzuka Pass == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group=Notes}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=阿部幹男|authorlink=阿部幹男|title=東北の田村語り|publisher=三弥井書店|series=三弥井民俗選書|date=2004-1|isbn=4-8382-9063-2|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=桐村英一郎|authorlink=桐村英一郎|title=熊野鬼伝説ー{{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} 英雄譚の誕生|publisher=三弥井書店|date=2012-1|isbn=4-8382-3221-7|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat}} * {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|ja|田村神社 (甲賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|1=Tamura Shrine (Koka City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576975}} * {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Shiroishi)|ja|田村神社 (白石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Shiroishi)|1=Tamura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350254}} * {{ill|Katayama Hiyoshi Shrine|ja|片山日子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Katayama Hiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569609}} == External Links == * [http://kameyama-kanko.com/area/sakasita/%e7%89%87%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-2/ Katayama Shrine (Kameyama City Tourism Association)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}}{{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|version=102979154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Katayama-Jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Katayama Shrine (Kameyama) (Q43594929)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Katayama shrine 01.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kameyama|WD=Q646858|1=Kameyama, Mie}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.89113888888889|N|136.33719444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} * * {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} (Also known as Suzuka Myojin or Suzuka Gongen) - Located in Kameyama City, Mie Prefecture. {{神社 |名称 = 片山神社 |image = [[File:Katayama shrine 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama City|1=Kameyama, Mie}}{{ill|Seki, Mie|en|Seki, Mie|WD=Q7447180|lt=Seki Town|1=Seki, Mie}}坂下636 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 53 | 緯度秒 = 28.1 | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 20 | 経度秒 = 13.9 |祭神 = {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.amigo.ne.jp/~chitonr2/jinjaichiran_kameyama.html|title=三重県の神社一覧 亀山市（20社）|publisher=三重県神社庁|accessdate=2018-2-9}}
{{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt_ja=瀬織津比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|lt_ja=氣吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86734190}} |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 鈴鹿大明神、鈴鹿明神、鈴鹿権現、鈴鹿神社、縣主の神社{{Refnest|group=注|『伊勢参宮名所図会』より}} |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Katayama shrine from Tōkaidō meisho zue.png|サムネイル|250px|right|東海道名所図会「鈴鹿社」]] '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} located at Sakasita 636, {{ill|Seki, Mie|en|Seki, Mie|WD=Q7447180|lt=Seki Town|1=Seki, Mie}}, {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama City|1=Kameyama, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. == Overview == It is situated on the {{ill|Suzuka Tunnel|ja|鈴鹿トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Tunnel|1=Suzuka tunnel (Shin-Meishin Expressway)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649475}} of {{ill|Japan National Route 1|en|Japan National Route 1|WD=Q1191191|lt=National Route 1|1=Japan National Route 1}}, which forms part of the border ({{ill|Prefectural Border|ja|県境|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Border|1=prefectural border|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582641}}) between {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, atop the {{ill|Suzuka Pass|ja|鈴鹿峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Pass|1=Suzuka Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649485}} from which one can overlook both the {{ill|Suzuka River|en|Suzuka River|WD=Q7651182|lt=Suzuka River|1=Suzuka River}} flowing toward {{ill|Ise Bay|en|Ise Bay|WD=Q1368308|lt=Ise Bay|1=Ise Bay}} and the {{ill|Tamura River|ja|田村川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura River|1=Tamura River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17172424}} flowing from {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}}{{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} The surrounding area is a sacred land blessed with abundant water. It is renowned as the site of the Suzuka Misogi, where the Saio ({{ill|Saiō|en|Saiō|WD=Q19841349|lt=Saio|1=Saiō}}) en route to {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} once stayed to perform misogi ({{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi|1=Misogi}}), and later became consecrated by shamanic practitioners ({{ill|Shamanic Practitioner|ja|巫覡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanic Practitioner|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu (shaman)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}}) performing purification rituals ({{ill|Purification Ritual|ja|祓え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祓|1=Harae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3127244}}). Within the precincts stand the monument commemorating the origin of Suzuka-ryu naginatajutsu – {{ill|Suzuka-ryu|ja|鈴鹿流|lt=Suzuka-ryu Naginatajutsu Origin|lt_ja=鈴鹿流薙刀術発生之地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka-ryu|1=Suzuka-ryū‎|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649496}} – and a monument honoring the filial piety of Bankichi. == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} ; Enshrined Deities * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt_ja=瀬織津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|lt_ja=氣吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86734190}} * {{ill|Hayasasurabime-no-kami|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurabime-no-kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Ooyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} == History == === Founding === The exact founding date is unknown. Originally, the three mountains of Mikoyama ({{ill|Suzuka-dake|ja|鈴鹿嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takenamada-dake|ja|武名嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenamada-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takahata-dake|ja|高幡嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) located on the eastern side of the Suzuka Pass were revered as a sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Sacred Mountain|1=Sacred mountains}}). On its central peak, {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} was enshrined, while on the left and right peaks {{ill|Ibuki-do-nushi|ja|伊吹戸主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibuki-do-nushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were respectively honored. However, a fire caused the transfer of the enshrinement to the old Suzuka Tondo Shrine. Subsequent repeated transfers due to frequent fires led to a unification of the shrine enshrining Yamato-hime – known as the Suzuka Shrine ({{ill|Suzuka Shrine|ja|鈴鹿社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – with the four deities, and later five additional deities including {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} were added. In the 2nd year of the Einin Era ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin Era|1=Einin}} 2, 1294), the shrine was relocated to the area of Tatsukami Saka and became the guardian shrine of Sakasita-juku ({{ill|Sakashita-juku|en|Sakashita-juku|WD=Q3033376|lt=Sakasita-juku|1=Sakashita-juku}}) enshrining Suzuka-hime. In the 40th year of the Meiji Era ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 40, 1907), the Tamura Shrine ({{ill|Tamura Shrine|ja|田村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine|qq=}}) near the Kagami Rock of Suzuka Pass was merged, forming the present structure. === General History === Due to its location on the border ({{ill|Provincial Border|ja|国境|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Border|1=State border|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12413618}}) between {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} and {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, {{ill|Suzuka Mountain|ja|鈴鹿山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Mountain|1=Q104539506|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539506}} was a key transportation hub since ancient times. Consequently, numerous legends concerning demons ({{ill|Oni|en|Oni|WD=Q730052|lt=Oni|1=Oni}}) and bandits ({{ill|Banditry|en|Banditry|WD=Q10968653|lt=Bandits|1=Banditry}}) have been handed down regarding the Suzuka Pass. Toward the end of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, the name “{{ill|Suzuka Gozen|en|Suzuka Gozen|WD=Q11649495|lt=Standing Eboshi|1=Suzuka Gozen}}” appears in the {{ill|Hobutsushu|en|Hobutsushu|WD=Q5875266|lt=Treasury Collection|1=Hobutsushu}}, and local legends relate that although {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} was ordered to subdue the Standing Eboshi, the two became husband and wife; after their deaths, the locals of the Suzuka Pass enshrined the Standing Eboshi as Suzuka Gomen and honored Tamuramaro in the Tamura Hall. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} In the {{ill|Nara Picture Book|ja|奈良絵本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Picture Book|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ‘Suzuka’, the Standing Eboshi of Suzuka is described as the Gongen of Suzuka, serving as the guardian deity ({{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}) of the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|WD=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (region)}}, protecting travelers in place of their own persons so that those journeying along the road would be spared misfortune. Furthermore, numerous small ritual vessels excavated in the vicinity of the Suzuka Pass suggest that travelers offered them in prayer for safe passage, indicating that the site functioned as a shrine festival remnant (and exhibiting characteristics of a Dosojin ({{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dosojin|1=Dōsojin}}) type deity). {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}} Much like the {{ill|Katsugun Jizō Bosatsu|ja|勝軍地蔵菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsugun Jizō Bosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}} enshrined on {{ill|Mount Atago|en|Mount Atago|WD=Q2975204|lt=Atago Mountain|1=Mount Atago}} in Kyoto, the Kagami Rock (also referred to as Kagamaseki) at the Suzuka Pass—regarded as an iwasaka ({{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwasaka|1=Iwakura rock}})—lies on the boundary between Kyoto ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|lt=Kyoto|lt_ja=京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}) and Tanba ({{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba|1=Tanba Province}}). There, by enshrining General Tamura within the rock, he was revered as a “general Jizō” in tandem with Suzuka Gongen, evidencing an ancient faith in Saino no Kami ({{ill|Saino no Kami|ja|塞の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saino no Kami|1=Q115978710|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q115978710}}). This belief later evolved into the legend of Sakanoue no Tamuramaro, as recounted in otogi-zōshi such as ‘Suzuka no Sōshi’ and ‘Standing Eboshi’, as well as in the {{ill|Okujōruri|ja|奥浄瑠璃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okujōruri|1=Okujoruri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361758}} text ‘Tamura Sandai-ki’, which narrates the tale of General Tamura and Suzuka Gomen as a divine couple battling the demon god ({{ill|Ootakemaru|en|Ootakemaru|WD=Q30926182|lt=Demon God|1=Ootakemaru}}). {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}} In the Shintō collection (‘{{ill|Shintōshū|en|Shintōshū|WD=Q2616931|lt=Shintō Shū|1=Shintōshū}}’), it is recorded that “the parents of Kakuwa-dono were also taught the principles of Shintō, and became the Suzuka Daimyōjin of the Iga Province Sannomiya”; however, since the Iga Sannomiya is identified with {{ill|Hataki Shrine|ja|波多岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this is suspected to be a misprint for the Ise Province Sannomiya. {{Refnest|group=注|『神道集』「児持山大明神の事」}} During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, the published work ‘{{ill|Ise Grand Shrine Famous Places Illustrated|ja|伊勢参宮名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine Famous Places Illustrated|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ in its “Suzuka Mountain” section depicts, separated by the Kagami Rock of the Suzuka Pass, a bolded “Suzuka Shrine” on the Ise side and, on the Ōmi side, a Tamura Myōjin (a shrine distinct from {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|ja|田村神社 (甲賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|1=Tamura Shrine (Koka City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576975}}), with an accompanying explanation stating that Suzuka Shrine was also known by alternate names such as Katayama Shrine and the bolded “Prefectural Shrine”. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} An inscribed stone pillar at the ruins of Tamura Shrine records that in the 40th year of the Meiji Era (1907) it was merged into Katayama Shrine; thus, the Tamura Shrine known as Tamura Myōjin during the Edo Period was incorporated into Katayama Shrine. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} In the 11th year of the Heisei Era (1999), the main hall was burned down due to arson. == Landmarks and Historic Sites == * Kagami Rock of Suzuka Mountain (Kagamaseki, Kagamihada) * The ruins of Tamura Shrine (also known as Tamura Hory or Tamura Myōjin) *: It is said that when Sakanoue no Tamuramaro deposited his bows and arrows ({{ill|Bows and Arrows|ja|弓箭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bows and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弓矢|1=Bow and arrow|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19827042}}), {{ill|Fumimuro Watamaro|ja|文室綿麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumimuro Watamaro|1=Fun'ya no Watamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2713178}} built a hall at that spot. {{Refnest|group=注|『三國地誌』巻之二十三 伊勢國 鈴鹿郡 神祠}} * The remains of the shrine festival site at Suzuka Pass == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group=Notes}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=阿部幹男|authorlink=阿部幹男|title=東北の田村語り|publisher=三弥井書店|series=三弥井民俗選書|date=2004-1|isbn=4-8382-9063-2|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=桐村英一郎|authorlink=桐村英一郎|title=熊野鬼伝説ー{{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} 英雄譚の誕生|publisher=三弥井書店|date=2012-1|isbn=4-8382-3221-7|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat}} * {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|ja|田村神社 (甲賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|1=Tamura Shrine (Koka City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576975}} * {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Shiroishi)|ja|田村神社 (白石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Shiroishi)|1=Tamura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350254}} * {{ill|Katayama Hiyoshi Shrine|ja|片山日子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Katayama Hiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569609}} == External Links == * [http://kameyama-kanko.com/area/sakasita/%e7%89%87%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-2/ Katayama Shrine (Kameyama City Tourism Association)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}}{{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|version=102979154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Katayama-Jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Katayama Shrine (Kameyama) (Q43594929)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|ja|鈴鹿郡|de|Suzuka-gun|zh|鈴鹿郡|lt=Suzuka district|WD=Q278828|1=Suzuka District, Mie}} || {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|lt=Katayama Shrine|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|commons|Category:Katayama-Jinja|WD=Q43594929|1=Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.891139|136.337194|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181266 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q43594929}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Katayama-Jinja]] [[da:d:Q43594929]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Katayama-Jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Kameyama, Mie]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kameyama, Mie]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamura storytelling]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Katayama shrine 01.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kameyama|WD=Q646858|1=Kameyama, Mie}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.89113888888889|N|136.33719444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} * * {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} (Also known as Suzuka Myojin or Suzuka Gongen) - Located in Kameyama City, Mie Prefecture. {{神社 |名称 = 片山神社 |image = [[File:Katayama shrine 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama City|1=Kameyama, Mie}}{{ill|Seki, Mie|en|Seki, Mie|WD=Q7447180|lt=Seki Town|1=Seki, Mie}}坂下636 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 53 | 緯度秒 = 28.1 | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 20 | 経度秒 = 13.9 |祭神 = {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.amigo.ne.jp/~chitonr2/jinjaichiran_kameyama.html|title=三重県の神社一覧 亀山市（20社）|publisher=三重県神社庁|accessdate=2018-2-9}}
{{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt_ja=瀬織津比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|lt_ja=氣吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86734190}} |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 鈴鹿大明神、鈴鹿明神、鈴鹿権現、鈴鹿神社、縣主の神社{{Refnest|group=注|『伊勢参宮名所図会』より}} |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Katayama shrine from Tōkaidō meisho zue.png|サムネイル|250px|right|東海道名所図会「鈴鹿社」]] '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} located at Sakasita 636, {{ill|Seki, Mie|en|Seki, Mie|WD=Q7447180|lt=Seki Town|1=Seki, Mie}}, {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama City|1=Kameyama, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. == Overview == It is situated on the {{ill|Suzuka Tunnel|ja|鈴鹿トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Tunnel|1=Suzuka tunnel (Shin-Meishin Expressway)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649475}} of {{ill|Japan National Route 1|en|Japan National Route 1|WD=Q1191191|lt=National Route 1|1=Japan National Route 1}}, which forms part of the border ({{ill|Prefectural Border|ja|県境|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Border|1=prefectural border|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582641}}) between {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, atop the {{ill|Suzuka Pass|ja|鈴鹿峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Pass|1=Suzuka Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649485}} from which one can overlook both the {{ill|Suzuka River|en|Suzuka River|WD=Q7651182|lt=Suzuka River|1=Suzuka River}} flowing toward {{ill|Ise Bay|en|Ise Bay|WD=Q1368308|lt=Ise Bay|1=Ise Bay}} and the {{ill|Tamura River|ja|田村川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura River|1=Tamura River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17172424}} flowing from {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}}{{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} The surrounding area is a sacred land blessed with abundant water. It is renowned as the site of the Suzuka Misogi, where the Saio ({{ill|Saiō|en|Saiō|WD=Q19841349|lt=Saio|1=Saiō}}) en route to {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} once stayed to perform misogi ({{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi|1=Misogi}}), and later became consecrated by shamanic practitioners ({{ill|Shamanic Practitioner|ja|巫覡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanic Practitioner|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu (shaman)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}}) performing purification rituals ({{ill|Purification Ritual|ja|祓え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祓|1=Harae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3127244}}). Within the precincts stand the monument commemorating the origin of Suzuka-ryu naginatajutsu – {{ill|Suzuka-ryu|ja|鈴鹿流|lt=Suzuka-ryu Naginatajutsu Origin|lt_ja=鈴鹿流薙刀術発生之地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka-ryu|1=Suzuka-ryū‎|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649496}} – and a monument honoring the filial piety of Bankichi. == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} ; Enshrined Deities * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt_ja=瀬織津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|lt_ja=氣吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86734190}} * {{ill|Hayasasurabime-no-kami|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurabime-no-kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Ooyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} == History == === Founding === The exact founding date is unknown. Originally, the three mountains of Mikoyama ({{ill|Suzuka-dake|ja|鈴鹿嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takenamada-dake|ja|武名嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenamada-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takahata-dake|ja|高幡嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) located on the eastern side of the Suzuka Pass were revered as a sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Sacred Mountain|1=Sacred mountains}}). On its central peak, {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} was enshrined, while on the left and right peaks {{ill|Ibuki-do-nushi|ja|伊吹戸主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibuki-do-nushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were respectively honored. However, a fire caused the transfer of the enshrinement to the old Suzuka Tondo Shrine. Subsequent repeated transfers due to frequent fires led to a unification of the shrine enshrining Yamato-hime – known as the Suzuka Shrine ({{ill|Suzuka Shrine|ja|鈴鹿社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – with the four deities, and later five additional deities including {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} were added. In the 2nd year of the Einin Era ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin Era|1=Einin}} 2, 1294), the shrine was relocated to the area of Tatsukami Saka and became the guardian shrine of Sakasita-juku ({{ill|Sakashita-juku|en|Sakashita-juku|WD=Q3033376|lt=Sakasita-juku|1=Sakashita-juku}}) enshrining Suzuka-hime. In the 40th year of the Meiji Era ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 40, 1907), the Tamura Shrine ({{ill|Tamura Shrine|ja|田村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine|qq=}}) near the Kagami Rock of Suzuka Pass was merged, forming the present structure. === General History === Due to its location on the border ({{ill|Provincial Border|ja|国境|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Border|1=State border|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12413618}}) between {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} and {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, {{ill|Suzuka Mountain|ja|鈴鹿山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Mountain|1=Q104539506|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539506}} was a key transportation hub since ancient times. Consequently, numerous legends concerning demons ({{ill|Oni|en|Oni|WD=Q730052|lt=Oni|1=Oni}}) and bandits ({{ill|Banditry|en|Banditry|WD=Q10968653|lt=Bandits|1=Banditry}}) have been handed down regarding the Suzuka Pass. Toward the end of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, the name “{{ill|Suzuka Gozen|en|Suzuka Gozen|WD=Q11649495|lt=Standing Eboshi|1=Suzuka Gozen}}” appears in the {{ill|Hobutsushu|en|Hobutsushu|WD=Q5875266|lt=Treasury Collection|1=Hobutsushu}}, and local legends relate that although {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} was ordered to subdue the Standing Eboshi, the two became husband and wife; after their deaths, the locals of the Suzuka Pass enshrined the Standing Eboshi as Suzuka Gomen and honored Tamuramaro in the Tamura Hall. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} In the {{ill|Nara Picture Book|ja|奈良絵本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Picture Book|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ‘Suzuka’, the Standing Eboshi of Suzuka is described as the Gongen of Suzuka, serving as the guardian deity ({{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}) of the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|WD=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (region)}}, protecting travelers in place of their own persons so that those journeying along the road would be spared misfortune. Furthermore, numerous small ritual vessels excavated in the vicinity of the Suzuka Pass suggest that travelers offered them in prayer for safe passage, indicating that the site functioned as a shrine festival remnant (and exhibiting characteristics of a Dosojin ({{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dosojin|1=Dōsojin}}) type deity). {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}} Much like the {{ill|Katsugun Jizō Bosatsu|ja|勝軍地蔵菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsugun Jizō Bosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}} enshrined on {{ill|Mount Atago|en|Mount Atago|WD=Q2975204|lt=Atago Mountain|1=Mount Atago}} in Kyoto, the Kagami Rock (also referred to as Kagamaseki) at the Suzuka Pass—regarded as an iwasaka ({{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwasaka|1=Iwakura rock}})—lies on the boundary between Kyoto ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|lt=Kyoto|lt_ja=京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}) and Tanba ({{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba|1=Tanba Province}}). There, by enshrining General Tamura within the rock, he was revered as a “general Jizō” in tandem with Suzuka Gongen, evidencing an ancient faith in Saino no Kami ({{ill|Saino no Kami|ja|塞の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saino no Kami|1=Q115978710|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q115978710}}). This belief later evolved into the legend of Sakanoue no Tamuramaro, as recounted in otogi-zōshi such as ‘Suzuka no Sōshi’ and ‘Standing Eboshi’, as well as in the {{ill|Okujōruri|ja|奥浄瑠璃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okujōruri|1=Okujoruri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361758}} text ‘Tamura Sandai-ki’, which narrates the tale of General Tamura and Suzuka Gomen as a divine couple battling the demon god ({{ill|Ootakemaru|en|Ootakemaru|WD=Q30926182|lt=Demon God|1=Ootakemaru}}). {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}} In the Shintō collection (‘{{ill|Shintōshū|en|Shintōshū|WD=Q2616931|lt=Shintō Shū|1=Shintōshū}}’), it is recorded that “the parents of Kakuwa-dono were also taught the principles of Shintō, and became the Suzuka Daimyōjin of the Iga Province Sannomiya”; however, since the Iga Sannomiya is identified with {{ill|Hataki Shrine|ja|波多岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this is suspected to be a misprint for the Ise Province Sannomiya. {{Refnest|group=注|『神道集』「児持山大明神の事」}} During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, the published work ‘{{ill|Ise Grand Shrine Famous Places Illustrated|ja|伊勢参宮名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine Famous Places Illustrated|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ in its “Suzuka Mountain” section depicts, separated by the Kagami Rock of the Suzuka Pass, a bolded “Suzuka Shrine” on the Ise side and, on the Ōmi side, a Tamura Myōjin (a shrine distinct from {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|ja|田村神社 (甲賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|1=Tamura Shrine (Koka City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576975}}), with an accompanying explanation stating that Suzuka Shrine was also known by alternate names such as Katayama Shrine and the bolded “Prefectural Shrine”. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} An inscribed stone pillar at the ruins of Tamura Shrine records that in the 40th year of the Meiji Era (1907) it was merged into Katayama Shrine; thus, the Tamura Shrine known as Tamura Myōjin during the Edo Period was incorporated into Katayama Shrine. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} In the 11th year of the Heisei Era (1999), the main hall was burned down due to arson. == Landmarks and Historic Sites == * Kagami Rock of Suzuka Mountain (Kagamaseki, Kagamihada) * The ruins of Tamura Shrine (also known as Tamura Hory or Tamura Myōjin) *: It is said that when Sakanoue no Tamuramaro deposited his bows and arrows ({{ill|Bows and Arrows|ja|弓箭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bows and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弓矢|1=Bow and arrow|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19827042}}), {{ill|Fumimuro Watamaro|ja|文室綿麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumimuro Watamaro|1=Fun'ya no Watamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2713178}} built a hall at that spot. {{Refnest|group=注|『三國地誌』巻之二十三 伊勢國 鈴鹿郡 神祠}} * The remains of the shrine festival site at Suzuka Pass == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group=Notes}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=阿部幹男|authorlink=阿部幹男|title=東北の田村語り|publisher=三弥井書店|series=三弥井民俗選書|date=2004-1|isbn=4-8382-9063-2|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=桐村英一郎|authorlink=桐村英一郎|title=熊野鬼伝説ー{{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} 英雄譚の誕生|publisher=三弥井書店|date=2012-1|isbn=4-8382-3221-7|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat}} * {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|ja|田村神社 (甲賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|1=Tamura Shrine (Koka City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576975}} * {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Shiroishi)|ja|田村神社 (白石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Shiroishi)|1=Tamura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350254}} * {{ill|Katayama Hiyoshi Shrine|ja|片山日子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Katayama Hiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569609}} == External Links == * [http://kameyama-kanko.com/area/sakasita/%e7%89%87%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-2/ Katayama Shrine (Kameyama City Tourism Association)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}}{{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|version=102979154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Katayama-Jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Katayama Shrine (Kameyama) (Q43594929)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|ja|鈴鹿郡|de|Suzuka-gun|zh|鈴鹿郡|lt=Suzuka district|WD=Q278828|1=Suzuka District, Mie}} || {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|lt=Katayama Shrine|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|commons|Category:Katayama-Jinja|WD=Q43594929|1=Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.891139|136.337194|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181266 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q43594929}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Katayama-Jinja]] [[da:d:Q43594929]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Katayama-Jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Kameyama, Mie]] [[Category:History of Kameyama, Mie]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamura storytelling]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Katayama shrine 01.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kameyama|WD=Q646858|1=Kameyama, Mie}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.89113888888889|N|136.33719444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} * * {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} (Also known as Suzuka Myojin or Suzuka Gongen) - Located in Kameyama City, Mie Prefecture. {{神社 |名称 = 片山神社 |image = [[File:Katayama shrine 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama City|1=Kameyama, Mie}}{{ill|Seki, Mie|en|Seki, Mie|WD=Q7447180|lt=Seki Town|1=Seki, Mie}}坂下636 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 53 | 緯度秒 = 28.1 | 経度度 = 136 | 経度分 = 20 | 経度秒 = 13.9 |祭神 = {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.amigo.ne.jp/~chitonr2/jinjaichiran_kameyama.html|title=三重県の神社一覧 亀山市（20社）|publisher=三重県神社庁|accessdate=2018-2-9}}
{{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt_ja=瀬織津比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|lt_ja=氣吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86734190}} |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 鈴鹿大明神、鈴鹿明神、鈴鹿権現、鈴鹿神社、縣主の神社{{Refnest|group=注|『伊勢参宮名所図会』より}} |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Katayama shrine from Tōkaidō meisho zue.png|サムネイル|250px|right|東海道名所図会「鈴鹿社」]] '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} located at Sakasita 636, {{ill|Seki, Mie|en|Seki, Mie|WD=Q7447180|lt=Seki Town|1=Seki, Mie}}, {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama City|1=Kameyama, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. == Overview == It is situated on the {{ill|Suzuka Tunnel|ja|鈴鹿トンネル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Tunnel|1=Suzuka tunnel (Shin-Meishin Expressway)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649475}} of {{ill|Japan National Route 1|en|Japan National Route 1|WD=Q1191191|lt=National Route 1|1=Japan National Route 1}}, which forms part of the border ({{ill|Prefectural Border|ja|県境|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Border|1=prefectural border|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582641}}) between {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, atop the {{ill|Suzuka Pass|ja|鈴鹿峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Pass|1=Suzuka Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649485}} from which one can overlook both the {{ill|Suzuka River|en|Suzuka River|WD=Q7651182|lt=Suzuka River|1=Suzuka River}} flowing toward {{ill|Ise Bay|en|Ise Bay|WD=Q1368308|lt=Ise Bay|1=Ise Bay}} and the {{ill|Tamura River|ja|田村川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura River|1=Tamura River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17172424}} flowing from {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}}{{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} The surrounding area is a sacred land blessed with abundant water. It is renowned as the site of the Suzuka Misogi, where the Saio ({{ill|Saiō|en|Saiō|WD=Q19841349|lt=Saio|1=Saiō}}) en route to {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} once stayed to perform misogi ({{ill|Misogi|en|Misogi|WD=Q1936265|lt=Misogi|1=Misogi}}), and later became consecrated by shamanic practitioners ({{ill|Shamanic Practitioner|ja|巫覡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanic Practitioner|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu (shaman)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}}) performing purification rituals ({{ill|Purification Ritual|ja|祓え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Purification Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祓|1=Harae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3127244}}). Within the precincts stand the monument commemorating the origin of Suzuka-ryu naginatajutsu – {{ill|Suzuka-ryu|ja|鈴鹿流|lt=Suzuka-ryu Naginatajutsu Origin|lt_ja=鈴鹿流薙刀術発生之地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka-ryu|1=Suzuka-ryū‎|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649496}} – and a monument honoring the filial piety of Bankichi. == Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} ; Enshrined Deities * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt_ja=瀬織津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|lt_ja=氣吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibikido-nushi-no-kami|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86734190}} * {{ill|Hayasasurabime-no-kami|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurabime-no-kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Ooyamazumi-no-kami|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamazumi-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} == History == === Founding === The exact founding date is unknown. Originally, the three mountains of Mikoyama ({{ill|Suzuka-dake|ja|鈴鹿嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takenamada-dake|ja|武名嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenamada-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Takahata-dake|ja|高幡嶽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata-dake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) located on the eastern side of the Suzuka Pass were revered as a sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Sacred Mountain|1=Sacred mountains}}). On its central peak, {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} was enshrined, while on the left and right peaks {{ill|Ibuki-do-nushi|ja|伊吹戸主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibuki-do-nushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were respectively honored. However, a fire caused the transfer of the enshrinement to the old Suzuka Tondo Shrine. Subsequent repeated transfers due to frequent fires led to a unification of the shrine enshrining Yamato-hime – known as the Suzuka Shrine ({{ill|Suzuka Shrine|ja|鈴鹿社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) – with the four deities, and later five additional deities including {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} were added. In the 2nd year of the Einin Era ({{ill|Einin|en|Einin|WD=Q1327307|lt=Einin Era|1=Einin}} 2, 1294), the shrine was relocated to the area of Tatsukami Saka and became the guardian shrine of Sakasita-juku ({{ill|Sakashita-juku|en|Sakashita-juku|WD=Q3033376|lt=Sakasita-juku|1=Sakashita-juku}}) enshrining Suzuka-hime. In the 40th year of the Meiji Era ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 40, 1907), the Tamura Shrine ({{ill|Tamura Shrine|ja|田村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine|qq=}}) near the Kagami Rock of Suzuka Pass was merged, forming the present structure. === General History === Due to its location on the border ({{ill|Provincial Border|ja|国境|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Border|1=State border|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12413618}}) between {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} and {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, {{ill|Suzuka Mountain|ja|鈴鹿山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Mountain|1=Q104539506|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539506}} was a key transportation hub since ancient times. Consequently, numerous legends concerning demons ({{ill|Oni|en|Oni|WD=Q730052|lt=Oni|1=Oni}}) and bandits ({{ill|Banditry|en|Banditry|WD=Q10968653|lt=Bandits|1=Banditry}}) have been handed down regarding the Suzuka Pass. Toward the end of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}, the name “{{ill|Suzuka Gozen|en|Suzuka Gozen|WD=Q11649495|lt=Standing Eboshi|1=Suzuka Gozen}}” appears in the {{ill|Hobutsushu|en|Hobutsushu|WD=Q5875266|lt=Treasury Collection|1=Hobutsushu}}, and local legends relate that although {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} was ordered to subdue the Standing Eboshi, the two became husband and wife; after their deaths, the locals of the Suzuka Pass enshrined the Standing Eboshi as Suzuka Gomen and honored Tamuramaro in the Tamura Hall. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} In the {{ill|Nara Picture Book|ja|奈良絵本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Picture Book|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ‘Suzuka’, the Standing Eboshi of Suzuka is described as the Gongen of Suzuka, serving as the guardian deity ({{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}) of the {{ill|Tōkaidō (region)|en|Tōkaidō (region)|WD=Q1196306|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (region)}}, protecting travelers in place of their own persons so that those journeying along the road would be spared misfortune. Furthermore, numerous small ritual vessels excavated in the vicinity of the Suzuka Pass suggest that travelers offered them in prayer for safe passage, indicating that the site functioned as a shrine festival remnant (and exhibiting characteristics of a Dosojin ({{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dosojin|1=Dōsojin}}) type deity). {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}} Much like the {{ill|Katsugun Jizō Bosatsu|ja|勝軍地蔵菩薩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsugun Jizō Bosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}} enshrined on {{ill|Mount Atago|en|Mount Atago|WD=Q2975204|lt=Atago Mountain|1=Mount Atago}} in Kyoto, the Kagami Rock (also referred to as Kagamaseki) at the Suzuka Pass—regarded as an iwasaka ({{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|WD=Q11587880|lt=Iwasaka|1=Iwakura rock}})—lies on the boundary between Kyoto ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|lt=Kyoto|lt_ja=京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}) and Tanba ({{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba|1=Tanba Province}}). There, by enshrining General Tamura within the rock, he was revered as a “general Jizō” in tandem with Suzuka Gongen, evidencing an ancient faith in Saino no Kami ({{ill|Saino no Kami|ja|塞の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saino no Kami|1=Q115978710|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q115978710}}). This belief later evolved into the legend of Sakanoue no Tamuramaro, as recounted in otogi-zōshi such as ‘Suzuka no Sōshi’ and ‘Standing Eboshi’, as well as in the {{ill|Okujōruri|ja|奥浄瑠璃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okujōruri|1=Okujoruri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361758}} text ‘Tamura Sandai-ki’, which narrates the tale of General Tamura and Suzuka Gomen as a divine couple battling the demon god ({{ill|Ootakemaru|en|Ootakemaru|WD=Q30926182|lt=Demon God|1=Ootakemaru}}). {{Sfn|阿部|2004|pages=88-93}} In the Shintō collection (‘{{ill|Shintōshū|en|Shintōshū|WD=Q2616931|lt=Shintō Shū|1=Shintōshū}}’), it is recorded that “the parents of Kakuwa-dono were also taught the principles of Shintō, and became the Suzuka Daimyōjin of the Iga Province Sannomiya”; however, since the Iga Sannomiya is identified with {{ill|Hataki Shrine|ja|波多岐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hataki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this is suspected to be a misprint for the Ise Province Sannomiya. {{Refnest|group=注|『神道集』「児持山大明神の事」}} During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, the published work ‘{{ill|Ise Grand Shrine Famous Places Illustrated|ja|伊勢参宮名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine Famous Places Illustrated|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’ in its “Suzuka Mountain” section depicts, separated by the Kagami Rock of the Suzuka Pass, a bolded “Suzuka Shrine” on the Ise side and, on the Ōmi side, a Tamura Myōjin (a shrine distinct from {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|ja|田村神社 (甲賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|1=Tamura Shrine (Koka City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576975}}), with an accompanying explanation stating that Suzuka Shrine was also known by alternate names such as Katayama Shrine and the bolded “Prefectural Shrine”. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} An inscribed stone pillar at the ruins of Tamura Shrine records that in the 40th year of the Meiji Era (1907) it was merged into Katayama Shrine; thus, the Tamura Shrine known as Tamura Myōjin during the Edo Period was incorporated into Katayama Shrine. {{Sfn|桐村|2012|pages=117-121}} In the 11th year of the Heisei Era (1999), the main hall was burned down due to arson. == Landmarks and Historic Sites == * Kagami Rock of Suzuka Mountain (Kagamaseki, Kagamihada) * The ruins of Tamura Shrine (also known as Tamura Hory or Tamura Myōjin) *: It is said that when Sakanoue no Tamuramaro deposited his bows and arrows ({{ill|Bows and Arrows|ja|弓箭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bows and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弓矢|1=Bow and arrow|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19827042}}), {{ill|Fumimuro Watamaro|ja|文室綿麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumimuro Watamaro|1=Fun'ya no Watamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2713178}} built a hall at that spot. {{Refnest|group=注|『三國地誌』巻之二十三 伊勢國 鈴鹿郡 神祠}} * The remains of the shrine festival site at Suzuka Pass == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group=Notes}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=阿部幹男|authorlink=阿部幹男|title=東北の田村語り|publisher=三弥井書店|series=三弥井民俗選書|date=2004-1|isbn=4-8382-9063-2|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=桐村英一郎|authorlink=桐村英一郎|title=熊野鬼伝説ー{{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} 英雄譚の誕生|publisher=三弥井書店|date=2012-1|isbn=4-8382-3221-7|ref=harv}} == See Also == {{commonscat}} * {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|ja|田村神社 (甲賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Kōka)|1=Tamura Shrine (Koka City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576975}} * {{ill|Tamura Shrine (Shiroishi)|ja|田村神社 (白石市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Shiroishi)|1=Tamura Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350254}} * {{ill|Katayama Hiyoshi Shrine|ja|片山日子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Katayama Hiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11569609}} == External Links == * [http://kameyama-kanko.com/area/sakasita/%e7%89%87%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be-2/ Katayama Shrine (Kameyama City Tourism Association)] {{Shinto navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}}{{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|version=102979154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Katayama-Jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Katayama Shrine (Kameyama) (Q43594929)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !!
